item.title: Soupedecode 01 CTF
item.description:
Máquina Soupedecode 01 Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción: Soupedecode es un desafío intenso y apasionante en el que los jugadores deben comprometer un controlador de dominio explotando la autenticación Kerberos, accediendo a recursos compartidos SMB, realizando ataques de fuerza bruta contra contraseñas y utilizando técnicas de Pass-the-Hash. Prepárate para poner a prueba tus habilidades y estrategias en esta aventura de ciberseguridad multifacética.

Enumeración Link to heading

item.title: Billing CTF
item.description:
Máquina Billing Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción: Algunos errores pueden resultar costosos.

Enumeración Link to heading

NMAP Link to heading

Comenzamos ejecutando un mapeo de puertos, luego scripts básicos de reconocimiento de servicios:

$ nmap -Pn -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 $target -oG nmap/allPorts -vvv
Starting Nmap 7.95 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-11 07:43 EST
PORT STATE SERVICE REASON
22/tcp open ssh syn-ack ttl 61
80/tcp open http syn-ack ttl 61
5038/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 61
$ nmap -Pn -p22,80,5038 -sCV $target -oN nmap/targeted
Starting Nmap 7.95 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-11-11 07:45 EST
PORT STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp open ssh OpenSSH 9.2p1 Debian 2+deb12u6 (protocol 2.0)
| ssh-hostkey: 
| 256 95:da:52:89:bd:77:c2:ca:9e:13:fd:68:e4:c2:d7:a5 (ECDSA)
|_ 256 40:a0:8c:9e:a7:00:2e:d8:de:f4:04:33:89:82:b1:a0 (ED25519)
80/tcp open http Apache httpd 2.4.62 ((Debian))
| http-title: MagnusBilling 
|_Requested resource was http://10.201.106.76/mbilling/
|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.62 (Debian)
| http-robots.txt: 1 disallowed entry 
|_/mbilling/
5038/tcp open asterisk Asterisk Call Manager 2.10.6
Service Info: OS: Linux; CPE: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel

3 servicios, y el último parece bastante interesante(Asterisk Call Manager).

item.title: Publisher CTF
item.description:
Máquina Publisher Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción: La máquina Publisher del CTF es un entorno simulado que aloja algunos servicios. Mediante diversas técnicas de enumeración, como el fuzzing de directorios y la identificación de versiones, se descubre una vulnerabilidad que permite la ejecución remota de código (RCE). Los intentos de escalar privilegios mediante un binario personalizado se ven obstaculizados por el acceso restringido a archivos y directorios críticos del sistema, lo que exige un análisis más profundo del perfil de seguridad del sistema para, en última instancia, explotar una vulnerabilidad que permite la ejecución de una shell bash sin restricciones y lograr la escalada de privilegios.

item.title: Blueprint CTF
item.description:
Máquina Blueprint Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción: Hackea esta máquina Windows y aumenta tus privilegios a Administrador.

Enumeración Link to heading

Realizamos un ping para constatar que la máquina esté activa:

$ ping -c1 10.201.112.210
PING 10.201.112.210 (10.201.112.210) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 10.201.112.210: icmp_seq=1 ttl=125 time=242 ms

--- 10.201.112.210 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 241.975/241.975/241.975/0.000 ms

NMAP Link to heading

Escaneamos los puertos y servicios que corren en esos puertos:

$ nmap -p- --open -sS --min-rate 5000 10.201.112.210 -oG nmap/allPorts -vvv
Starting Nmap 7.95 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2025-10-18 15:15 EDT
Initiating Ping Scan at 15:15
Scanning 10.201.112.210 [4 ports]
Completed Ping Scan at 15:15, 0.54s elapsed (1 total hosts)
Initiating Parallel DNS resolution of 1 host. at 15:15
Completed Parallel DNS resolution of 1 host. at 15:15, 0.04s elapsed
DNS resolution of 1 IPs took 0.04s. Mode: Async [#: 1, OK: 0, NX: 1, DR: 0, SF: 0, TR: 1, CN: 0]
Initiating SYN Stealth Scan at 15:15
Scanning 10.201.112.210 [65535 ports]
Discovered open port 139/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 3306/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 445/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 443/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 80/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 8080/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 135/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49158/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49154/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49153/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49152/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49160/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Discovered open port 49159/tcp on 10.201.112.210
Completed SYN Stealth Scan at 15:16, 49.08s elapsed (65535 total ports)
Nmap scan report for 10.201.112.210
Host is up, received timestamp-reply ttl 125 (0.42s latency).
Scanned at 2025-10-18 15:15:36 EDT for 49s
Not shown: 49151 filtered tcp ports (no-response), 16371 closed tcp ports (reset)
Some closed ports may be reported as filtered due to --defeat-rst-ratelimit
PORT STATE SERVICE REASON
80/tcp open http syn-ack ttl 125
135/tcp open msrpc syn-ack ttl 125
139/tcp open netbios-ssn syn-ack ttl 125
443/tcp open https syn-ack ttl 125
445/tcp open microsoft-ds syn-ack ttl 125
3306/tcp open mysql syn-ack ttl 125
8080/tcp open http-proxy syn-ack ttl 125
49152/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
49153/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
49154/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
49158/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
49159/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
49160/tcp open unknown syn-ack ttl 125
$ cat nmap/targeted 
# Nmap 7.95 scan initiated Sat Oct 18 15:17:06 2025 as: /usr/lib/nmap/nmap --privileged -p80,135,139,443,445,3306,8080,49152,49153,49154,49158,49159,49160 -sCV -oN nmap/targeted 10.201.112.210
Nmap scan report for 10.201.112.210
Host is up (0.44s latency).

PORT STATE SERVICE VERSION
80/tcp open http Microsoft HTTPAPI httpd 2.0 (SSDP/UPnP)
|_http-title: 404 - File or directory not found.
| http-methods: 
|_ Potentially risky methods: TRACE
|_http-server-header: Microsoft-IIS/7.5
135/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
139/tcp open netbios-ssn Microsoft Windows netbios-ssn
443/tcp open ssl/http Apache httpd 2.4.23 (OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.28)
| tls-alpn: 
|_ http/1.1
|_ssl-date: TLS randomness does not represent time
| ssl-cert: Subject: commonName=localhost
| Not valid before: 2009-11-10T23:48:47
|_Not valid after: 2019-11-08T23:48:47
| http-methods: 
|_ Potentially risky methods: TRACE
|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.23 (Win32) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.28
|_http-title: Bad request!
445/tcp open microsoft-ds Windows 7 Home Basic 7601 Service Pack 1 microsoft-ds (workgroup: WORKGROUP)
3306/tcp open mysql MariaDB 10.3.23 or earlier (unauthorized)
8080/tcp open http Apache httpd 2.4.23 (OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.28)
| http-methods: 
|_ Potentially risky methods: TRACE
| http-ls: Volume /
| SIZE TIME FILENAME
| - 2019-04-11 22:52 oscommerce-2.3.4/
| - 2019-04-11 22:52 oscommerce-2.3.4/catalog/
| - 2019-04-11 22:52 oscommerce-2.3.4/docs/
|_
|_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.23 (Win32) OpenSSL/1.0.2h PHP/5.6.28
|_http-title: Index of /
49152/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
49153/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
49154/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
49158/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
49159/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
49160/tcp open msrpc Microsoft Windows RPC
Service Info: Hosts: www.example.com, BLUEPRINT, localhost; OS: Windows; CPE: cpe:/o:microsoft:windows

Host script results:
|_nbstat: NetBIOS name: BLUEPRINT, NetBIOS user: <unknown>, NetBIOS MAC: 16:ff:dd:9b:b0:03 (unknown)
| smb-security-mode: 
| account_used: guest
| authentication_level: user
| challenge_response: supported
|_ message_signing: disabled (dangerous, but default)
| smb2-time: 
| date: 2025-10-18T19:18:15
|_ start_date: 2025-10-18T19:11:36
| smb2-security-mode: 
| 2:1:0: 
|_ Message signing enabled but not required
| smb-os-discovery: 
| OS: Windows 7 Home Basic 7601 Service Pack 1 (Windows 7 Home Basic 6.1)
| OS CPE: cpe:/o:microsoft:windows_7::sp1
| Computer name: BLUEPRINT
| NetBIOS computer name: BLUEPRINT/x00
| Workgroup: WORKGROUP/x00
|_ System time: 2025-10-18T20:18:16+01:00
|_clock-skew: mean: -19m59s, deviation: 34m35s, median: -1s

Observando los servidores http: Link to heading

item.title: Cap CTF
item.description:
Máquina Cap Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción:

Enumración Link to heading

Hacemos un ping al host para ver si está activo e identificar el S.O a través del TTL(No es 100% confiable el TTL, pero bueno):

$ ping -c1 10.10.10.245 
PING 10.10.10.245 (10.10.10.245) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 10.10.10.245: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=84.2 ms

--- 10.10.10.245 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 84.188/84.188/84.188/0.000 ms

Está activa y es una Linux

NMAP Link to heading

Hacemos un escaneo de puertos y ejecutar scripts básicos de reconocimiento de servicios:

item.title: Bugged CTF
item.description:
Máquina Bugged Link to heading

Dificultad: Fácil

Descripción: John estaba trabajando en sus electrodomésticos inteligentes cuando notó tráfico extraño en la red. ¿Puedes ayudarle a descubrir qué son estas comunicaciones de red extrañas?

Enumeración Link to heading

Comenzamos haciendo un ping a la máquina objetivo():

PING 10.201.64.81 (10.201.64.81) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 10.201.64.81: icmp_seq=1 ttl=61 time=229 ms

--- 10.201.64.81 ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 228.641/228.641/228.641/0.000 ms

Podemos determinar que es un S.O Linux a través del TTL(OJO: esto se puede modificar).
